payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

original_descriptions: Pocket money as the strategic monetary reward entity

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Task claiming process enabling children to register completed work
payload.justification: Enabling children to claim completed tasks (SG910) describes a strategic process for work registration.

payload.description: Task definition process for creating rewardable work assignments
payload.justification: Parental activity to define rewardable tasks with monetary values (SG911) represents a strategic process.

payload.description: Pocket money distribution process based on earned tasks
payload.justification: Facilitating accurate pocket money distribution based on earned tasks (SG914) is a core strategic process.

payload.description: Reporting process for tracking earnings and task completion
payload.justification: Parental need for reporting (SG914) describes a strategic reporting process.

payload.description: Visibility provision function for task and earnings transparency
payload.justification: Providing visibility into tasks (SG912) and earnings transparency (SG913) represent strategic functions.

payload.description: Historical record-keeping function for accountability tracking
payload.justification: Record-keeping for tracking or accountability (SG915) represents a strategic archival function.

payload.description: Task as a strategic unit of rewardable work for children
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference tasks as the central entity: completed tasks claimed for pocket money (SG910), tasks defined with monetary values (SG911), and visibility into available/completed tasks (SG912). This is clearly a core strategic entity.

payload.description: Earnings/pocket money as the strategic financial tracking and reward entity
payload.justification: Pocket money is explicitly mentioned as the purpose (SG910) and distribution mechanism (SG914), representing the financial reward entity central to the enterprise. | Weekly earnings transparency (SG913) indicates earnings as a distinct strategic entity for financial awareness.

payload.description: Historical record as a strategic accountability artifact
payload.justification: Historical record-keeping (SG915) represents a strategic entity for tracking and accountability purposes.

payload.description: Monetary value associated with tasks
payload.justification: Tasks with associated monetary values (SG911) indicates monetary value as a strategic attribute of tasks.

payload.description: Task completion status
payload.justification: Completed tasks (SG910) and visibility into completed tasks (SG912) indicate completion status as a strategic attribute.

payload.description: Tasks earned by children resulting in pocket money
payload.justification: The relationship between completed tasks and pocket money distribution (SG910, SG914) is a strategic business relationship.

payload.description: Weekly pocket money distribution cycle
payload.justification: Tasks claimed throughout the week (SG910) and weekly earnings transparency (SG913) indicate a strategic weekly cycle.

payload.description: Child as primary task performer and earnings recipient
payload.justification: Children claim tasks (SG910), require visibility (SG912), and benefit from earnings transparency (SG913), identifying them as a strategic actor.

payload.description: Parent as task administrator and reward distributor
payload.justification: Parents define tasks (SG911) and require reporting for distribution (SG914), identifying them as a strategic actor.

payload.description: Enable children to earn pocket money through task completion
payload.justification: Core purpose of enabling children to claim tasks for pocket money (SG910) is a primary strategic goal.

payload.description: Support children's financial awareness development
payload.justification: Weekly earnings transparency to support financial awareness (SG913) is a strategic educational goal.

payload.description: Motivate children through visibility of available opportunities
payload.justification: Motivational goal of providing task visibility (SG912) is a strategic engagement goal.

payload.description: Ensure accurate and fair reward distribution
payload.justification: Facilitating accurate pocket money distribution (SG914) represents a strategic fairness goal.

payload.description: Maintain accountability through historical tracking
payload.justification: Historical record-keeping for accountability (SG915) represents a strategic governance goal.

payload.description: Transparency as a guiding principle for task and earnings visibility
payload.justification: Visibility into tasks (SG912) and earnings transparency (SG913) reflect a strategic principle of openness.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Family Participation Structure
payload.description: Defines the enterprise concern of who participates in the household economy, establishing the fundamental roles and relationships between family members in the earned allowance system.

payload.name: Developmental Accountability
payload.description: Encompasses the enterprise commitment to children's financial education and behavioral development through structured work-reward mechanisms, establishing why the household economy exists beyond simple compensation.

payload.name: Contribution Recognition Framework
payload.description: Represents the enterprise obligation to define, track, and acknowledge work contributions by household participants, establishing what constitutes rewardable effort and how completion is validated.

payload.name: Economic Obligation Fulfillment
payload.description: Governs the enterprise responsibility for fair and accurate compensation distribution, ensuring earned entitlements are properly calculated, recorded, and delivered to participants on committed schedules.

payload.name: Transparency Governance
payload.description: Establishes the enterprise principle that all participants have visibility into opportunities, performance, and outcomes, ensuring information asymmetry does not undermine trust or developmental goals.

payload.name: Historical Accountability Preservation
payload.description: Addresses the enterprise concern for maintaining auditable records of participation, contribution, and compensation over time, supporting both dispute resolution and longitudinal developmental assessment.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise the child as the primary task performer and earnings recipient.
payload.rationale: Establishes the fundamental structural relationship of the child participant within the household economy scope, defining their dual role as both performer and recipient.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise the parent as the task administrator and reward distributor.
payload.rationale: Establishes the fundamental structural relationship of the parent participant within the household economy scope, defining their dual role as both administrator and distributor.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to earn pocket money through structured task completion.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise's governance commitment to a work-reward mechanism as a foundational policy constraint, derived from Why-column motivation content.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall support the development of children's financial awareness.
payload.rationale: Expresses an enterprise-level commitment to developmental outcomes, functioning as a policy constraint that governs the purpose of the household economy.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide visibility of available earning opportunities to motivate children.
payload.rationale: While derived from Why-column motivation, the obligation centers on providing a capability (visibility) to achieve a motivational outcome, making it functionally oriented at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a framework by which household participants may register completed work contributions for recognition.
payload.rationale: Task claiming process represents an enterprise commitment to enable participant work registration, expressed as an enterprise-scope capability rather than system mechanics.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a process for defining rewardable work assignments within the household.
payload.rationale: Task definition process reflects the enterprise's obligation to create structured work opportunities, framed at strategic governance level.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise work contributions as discrete units eligible for reward.
payload.rationale: Task entity represents a foundational structural element of the contribution framework—the enterprise commits to defining work at this granularity.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall associate monetary value with each unit of rewardable work.
payload.rationale: Monetary value attribute represents the enterprise's commitment to quantify the economic worth of contributions, essential to the reward mechanism.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain the completion status of each work contribution.
payload.rationale: Completion status attribute represents the enterprise's obligation to track lifecycle state for validation and reward eligibility determination.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall distribute pocket money based on earned tasks.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise commitment to compensate participants based on completed tasks, addressing both the distribution process and the causal relationship between task completion and earnings.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain pocket money as a financial tracking and reward entity.
payload.rationale: Defines the enterprise's structural commitment to recognize pocket money as a core entity for both tracking financial obligations and rewarding participants.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall distribute pocket money on a weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: Commits the enterprise to a temporal obligation—regular weekly distribution—derived from the When-column cycle event.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall ensure accurate and fair reward distribution.
payload.rationale: Expresses a governance constraint derived from the Why-column goal, establishing a normative rule for accuracy and fairness in compensation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish transparency as a guiding principle for all participant activities and outcomes.
payload.rationale: Transparency principle from Why-column provides normative governance constraint at enterprise scope

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide visibility into task opportunities and performance to all participants.
payload.rationale: Visibility provision function derived from How-column CCI, expressed as enterprise commitment to provide transparency capability

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain reporting processes for tracking earnings and task completion outcomes.
payload.rationale: Reporting process obligation derived from How-column CCI, expressed as enterprise accountability for maintaining reporting capability

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain auditable records of participation, contribution, and compensation over time.
payload.rationale: Consolidates the enterprise commitment to preserve historical accountability artifacts (What), the function of maintaining those records (How), and the motivating goal of accountability through historical tracking (Why) into a single enterprise-level obligation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall ensure historical records support dispute resolution and longitudinal developmental assessment.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise's commitment to the purpose and use constraints governing historical records — that they must be fit for specific accountability purposes rather than simply existing.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Requirement elements present in the ledger.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the core purpose of enabling children to claim completed tasks for pocket money throughout the week.
Articulates parental motivation to define rewardable tasks with associated monetary values.
Captures the motivational goal of providing children visibility into available and completed tasks.
States the purpose of weekly earnings transparency to support financial awareness for children.
Defines parental need for reporting to facilitate accurate pocket money distribution based on earned tasks.
Indicates strategic requirement for historical record-keeping to support tracking or accountability purposes.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Signal elements present in the ledger.
This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Stakeholder elements present in the ledger.
